zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane de Borgate於1254年出生在英國時，她的父親William Borgate54歲，母親Borgate34歲。她和Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane de Borgate于1254年出生在英国时，她的父亲William Borgate54岁，母亲Borgate34岁。她和Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Borgate
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from one or more of the many places called Northgate, such as Northgate Hall in Warham (Norfolk), Northgate Farm in Ellisfield (Hants), and Northgate in Pinchbeck (Lincs). The place-names derive from Middle English north ‘north, northern’ + gate ‘gate’ or gate ‘path, road’ (Old English norð, Old English geat ‘gate’, Old Scandinavian gata ‘path’).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane de Borgate was born in 1254, in England, her father, William Borgate, was 54 and her mother, Borgate, was 34. She had at least 1 son with Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane de Borgate est née en 1254, à Angleterre, son père, William Borgate, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Borgate, avait 34 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane de Borgate 1254 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Borgate 54 und ihre Mutter Borgate 34. Sie hatte mit Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de Borgate è nata il nel 1254 nel England, sua padre, William Borgate, aveva 54 anni e sua madre, Borgate, aveva 34 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane de Borgateが1254年にイギリスで生まれた時，父William Borgateは54歳，母Borgateは34歳であった。彼女はSir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane de Borgate(이)가 1254년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Borgate(은)는 54세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Borgate(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de Borgate nasceu em 1254, em Inglaterra, seu pai, William Borgate, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Borgate, tinha 34 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane de Borgate родилась в 1254 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени William Borgate было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Borgate, 34. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane de Borgate nació en 1254, en Inglaterra, su papá, William Borgate, tenía 54 años y su mamá, Borgate, tenía 34 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sir Edmond Edward Tyrrell of Avon.
